sections_added: Key Files Explanation (AndroidManifest.xml, MainActivity.kt, activity_main.xml, build.gradle.kts)

patterns_discovered.description: Documentation follows numbered step format with detailed explanations and substeps
Each configuration field is explained with purpose, examples, guidelines, and important notes
Instructions differentiate between Windows, macOS, and Linux path configurations
Project structure presented as ASCII tree with directory descriptions and file purposes
Each recommendation (template choice, SDK version, language) includes 'Why' explanation
Internal links to prerequisites, troubleshooting sections, and related topics

gotchas_discovered.description: Windows 260 character path limit mentioned; need to avoid long paths and special characters
First Gradle sync may take 5-15 minutes; user may think build is hung
Package name cannot be changed after publishing to Google Play Store
Two competing build systems (Kotlin DSL vs Groovy DSL) with different advantages
Different templates have varying boilerplate and setup requirements

recommendations.description: Include screenshots or diagrams of Android Studio wizard steps for better visual clarity
Create dedicated troubleshooting section for common Gradle sync issues, path problems, and dependency conflicts
Consider adding links to video tutorials for users who prefer visual learning
Add notes about SDK version compatibility and when to update minimum/target SDK versions
Extract key configuration values into a quick reference table for fast lookup
Extend documentation with basic testing setup and verification steps after project creation
